headline: Russian-speaking attackers have compromised credentials on 86,644 internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPNs as CISA urges every FortiGate operator to lock down.

emerging_trends.text: Stolen credentials and leaked secrets anchor the day's biggest incidents, from FortiBleed's 86,644 compromised Fortinet devices to API keys bleeding out of a WordPress mail plugin on 100,000 sites.
Researchers keep turning autonomous AI agents into liabilities, with the AutoJack chain showing how a single web page rides an AI browsing agent into remote code execution on its host.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsers Into Host RCE

emerging_trends.text: Vendors are racing to consolidate, with Accenture and Cisco both spending to bolt operational-technology and agentic-SOC tooling onto their portfolios.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Accenture and Cisco Stack OT and SOC Deals

topics.title: FortiBleed Hits 86,644 Fortinet Devices
topics.latest_developments: CISA urged every FortiGate operator to secure devices after the FortiBleed campaign, pinned on Russian-speaking actors, compromised credentials on 86,644 internet-facing Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways, roughly half the appliances reachable online.
topics.summary: FortiBleed is a large-scale credential-theft campaign exposing login data for tens of thousands of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls and VPN gateways across government and private organizations. Operators should rotate all credentials, pull management interfaces off the internet, and hunt their devices for unauthorized access.

sources.title: CISA Warns Fortinet Customers as FortiBleed Hits 86,644 FortiGate Devices
CISA warns Fortinet users to secure devices after FortiBleed leak

topics.title: AutoJack Turns AI Browsers Into Host RCE
topics.latest_developments: Microsoft researchers disclosed AutoJack, an exploit chain in which a single malicious web page steers an AI browsing agent into reaching a privileged localhost service, AutoGen Studio's MCP WebSocket, and spawning a process on the host with no credentials and no further user interaction.
topics.summary: AutoJack abuses an AI browsing agent's trust in localhost, missing authentication, and unsafe parameter handling to turn ordinary web content into host remote code execution. Teams running agentic AI should isolate local services and require authentication on MCP endpoints.

sources.title: AutoJack Attack Lets One Web Page Hijack AI Agent for Host Code Execution
AutoJack: How a single page can RCE the host running your AI agent

topics.title: Accenture and Cisco Stack OT and SOC Deals
topics.latest_developments: Cisco announced it will acquire WideField Security to extend Splunk's agentic SOC into identity, credentials, sessions, and blast radius, the same week Help Net Security pegged Accenture's purchase of a majority stake in Dragos plus all of runZero and NetRise at $4.2 billion.
topics.summary: Two large vendors are consolidating defensive tooling: Accenture is assembling end-to-end operational-technology security across Dragos, runZero, and NetRise, and Cisco is buying WideField to broaden Splunk threat investigation. The deals concentrate OT and SOC capability under fewer providers.

sources.title: Accenture to buy Dragos, runZero, and NetRise in $4.2 billion cybersecurity deal
Cisco to Acquire WideField Security to Boost Splunk's Agentic SOC

topics.title: Gravity SMTP Plugin Bleeds API Keys
topics.latest_developments: Threat actors began exploiting CVE-2026-4020, a CVSS 5.3 unauthenticated information-disclosure flaw in the Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin installed on about 100,000 sites, to pull configuration data including API keys, secrets, and OAuth tokens.
topics.summary: Gravity SMTP, a WordPress mail-delivery plugin, leaks sensitive configuration to unauthenticated attackers, handing them keys and tokens for connected services. Site owners should install the patched release at once and rotate every exposed secret.

sources.title: Hackers exploit info disclosure bug in Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin

topics.title: Imposter Scams Cost Americans $3.5 Billion
topics.latest_developments: The Federal Trade Commission reported that imposter scams, in which fraudsters pose as a victim's bank, a government agency, or a local planning office, drained $3.5 billion from Americans in 2025, and the losses keep climbing.
topics.summary: Imposter scams impersonate trusted institutions to trick people into sending money or credentials, and they now rank among the costliest consumer frauds in the United States. Consumers should verify any urgent payment or account request through an independently known phone number.

sources.title: Imposter scams cost Americans $3.5 billion in 2025 – and it's getting worse

topics.title: Novo Nordisk Token Leak Exposes Pipeline Risk
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading traced the Novo Nordisk breach to a single leaked GitHub token, framing the failure as an identity problem at heart and exposing how software-development pipelines widen the attack surface.
topics.summary: A leaked GitHub token gave attackers a path into Novo Nordisk's development environment, showing that machine credentials and secrets are themselves identities. Organizations should scope, rotate, and monitor secrets the way they govern human accounts.

sources.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk

weather: This Afternoon: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms, high 80F.
Tonight: Chance Showers And Thunderstorms then Mostly Clear, low 58F.

games.recap: Fulford's pinch 2-run double in 8th lifts Rockies past Pirates 4-3, Freeland reaches 1,000 Ks

business_politics.title: Iran Closes Hormuz as Lebanon Fighting Flares
business_politics.latest_developments: Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again Saturday over Israel's strikes in Lebanon, after ships had begun transiting under the interim accord; Israel and Hezbollah then agreed to a renewed ceasefire, the postponed U.S.-Iran talks were set back on for Sunday in Switzerland, and Trump warned he could levy U.S. tolls on Strait traffic absent a final deal within 60 days.
business_politics.summary: Iran's joint military command said it shut the Strait of Hormuz, the chokepoint for roughly a fifth of the world's seaborne oil, citing Israeli attacks in Lebanon, undercutting the interim deal Washington and Tehran signed days earlier; negotiators still head to Switzerland for talks meant to reach a final agreement within 60 days.

sources.title: Iran Says It Closed Strait of Hormuz as Fighting Flares Again in Lebanon
Israel, Hezbollah Agree to Renewed Ceasefire as Iran Peace Talks Postponed
What's Next for Trump's Iran Deal?

business.title: Universities Press Harrisburg on Funding
business.latest_developments: Penn State president Neeli Bendapudi, Temple president John Fry, and Pitt chancellor Joan Gabel jointly urged the legislature to fund Pennsylvania's new performance-based higher-education model before the June 30 budget deadline.
business.summary: Pennsylvania's three state-related universities—Penn State, Temple, and the University of Pittsburgh—want lawmakers to bankroll a new performance-based funding formula this year, arguing the commonwealth's workforce, economy, and long-term competitiveness hinge on it as the June 30, 2026, budget deadline nears.

sources.title: Neeli Bendapudi, John Fry and Joan Gabel: Fund higher ed accountability now or lose competitive edge

around_town.title: Pitt-WVU Backyard Basketball Classic
around_town.latest_developments: The University of Pittsburgh and West Virginia University will meet in a Backyard Basketball Classic, with ticket information and game times due later this summer.
around_town.summary: Pitt and West Virginia will revive their Backyard Brawl rivalry on the hardwood in a Backyard Basketball Classic; organizers plan to announce dates, tip times, and ticket details later this summer.

sources.title: Pitt to Play West Virginia in Backyard Basketball Classic

events.title: Dillcathalon Returns to Picklesburgh
events.latest_developments: Sign-ups opened for the Dillcathalon Games, the pickle-themed competition returning to Pittsburgh's downtown Picklesburgh festival.
events.summary: The Dillcathalon Games, a slate of fan-favorite pickle-themed contests, will return to Picklesburgh, the downtown Pittsburgh food festival, and organizers are now taking competitor sign-ups.

sources.title: Dillcathalon Games returning to Picklesburgh. Here's how you can sign up to compete

around_teams.title: Pirates' Catcher Plan After Bart Trade
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette mailbag laid out the Pirates' catching plan following the Joey Bart trade, pointing to Henry Davis behind the plate and weighing whether designated hitter Marcell Ozuna returns.
around_teams.summary: After dealing catcher Joey Bart to the Atlanta Braves for reliever Hunter Stratton, the Pittsburgh Pirates lean on Henry Davis at catcher, the Post-Gazette reported, as readers asked whether Marcell Ozuna stays with the club.

sources.title: Pirates mailbag: What's the plan at catcher after the Joey Bart trade? Is Marcell Ozuna back?

around_teams.title: Rookie Safety's 4.32 Forty
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette feature detailed how Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings reshaped his draft stock with a 4.32-second 40-yard dash at the scouting combine.
around_teams.summary: Pittsburgh Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings vaulted up draft boards after running the 40-yard dash in 4.32 seconds at the NFL combine, the Post-Gazette reported in a look at how the time changed his trajectory.

sources.title: How Steelers rookie safety Robert Spears-Jennings changed his NFL trajectory in 4.32 seconds

team_usa.title: Freeman's Full-Circle Goal for U.S.
team_usa.latest_developments: Right back Alex Freeman scored in the United States' 2-0 win over Australia in Seattle, a goal sharpened by family symmetry: his father, former Green Bay Packers receiver Antonio Freeman, once starred at the same venue.
team_usa.summary: Defender Alex Freeman scored as the United States men's national team beat Australia 2-0 in Seattle on Friday, June 19, securing the top spot in World Cup Group D and a place in the round of 32; his father, Antonio Freeman, played receiver for the Packers at that stadium.

sources.title: Freeman goal for U.S. 'full circle family moment'

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Zitron extends his case that Silicon Valley sits inside an AI bubble, building on his scoop that OpenAI spent $34 billion to generate $13.07 billion in 2025 revenue, and argues such companies have no credible path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: An Interview with Michael Morton About E-Commerce in the Age of AI
reading.summary: Ben Thompson interviews Michael Morton on how AI reshapes e-commerce, covering unfalsifiable bear cases, distribution versus referral models, grocery, and autonomous vehicles.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview with the Work AI Institute's Rebecca Hinds, Newport argues that overload and distraction had already broken knowledge work long before AI arrived.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
